content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a mobile game. In the game, Chef's character Chefario can perform special attacks. However, one special attack costs XXX mana points to Chefario.

If Chefario currently has YYY mana points, determine the **maximum** number of special attacks he can perform.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the cost of one special attack and the number of mana points Chefario has initially.


Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum number of special attacks Chefario can perform.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y – the mana cost of one special attack and the initial mana points respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the maximum number of special attacks Chefario can perform.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 30/10 = 3 attacks.\nTest case 2: floor(41/6) = 6 attacks.\nTest case 3: 2 < 50 so 0 attacks.

solution.approach: For each test case the answer is simply the integer division Y // X because each attack consumes exactly X mana points and attacks are independent. Compute ans = Y / X using integer division (floor).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time division per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it)); Y = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(Y//X))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
